content: DOW THEORY — SIX PRINCIPLES: 1) Market Discounts Everything. 2) Three Trends: Primary (months-years), Secondary (weeks-months, 33-66% retracement), Minor (days-weeks). 3) Primary Has Three Phases: Accumulation, Public Participation, Distribution. 4) Indices Must Confirm Each Other (industrials + transports). 5) Volume Confirms Trend. 6) Trend Intact Until Definitive Reversal. Primary bull signal: BOTH industrials and transports close above prior significant high.

VOLUME — ACCUMULATION/DISTRIBUTION DAY: Up day with higher volume than prior up day = accumulation. Down day with higher volume than prior down day = distribution. Track: 3-5 consecutive distribution days near top = institutional selling. 3-5 accumulation days near bottom = buying.

MARKET PHASE IDENTIFICATION — Phase 1 (Accumulation): low vol, tight range, low volume, bearish sentiment. Phase 2 (Markup): rising prices, expanding volume. Phase 3 (Distribution): high vol, wide range, extreme volume, euphoria. Phase 4 (Markdown): declining prices, expanding volume on declines. Each phase needs different strategy.

TREND DEFINITION — Uptrend: higher highs + higher lows. Downtrend: lower highs + lower lows. Reversal requires violation of prior swing low (uptrend) or swing high (downtrend). Minor trend = days-weeks. Intermediate = weeks-months. Primary = months-years. Each level analyzed separately - minor reversal does NOT imply primary change.
ROLE REVERSAL: Broken support becomes resistance. Broken resistance becomes support. This principle is fundamental and applies at all timeframes. The break must be decisive with volume.

HEAD AND SHOULDERS TOP — Most reliable reversal. Left Shoulder (high volume), Head (lower volume), Right Shoulder (much lower volume). NECKLINE connects two lows between head/shoulders. CONFIRMATION: close below neckline on EXPANDED volume. TARGET: head-to-neckline distance projected downward from breakout. Declining volume across shoulders = critical confirmation.

PATTERN RELIABILITY (Statistical) — H&S top: ~83% success on neckline break with volume. Inverse H&S: ~81%. Double top: ~72%. Ascending triangle: ~77% bullish. Descending: ~73% bearish. Symmetrical: ~75% continuation. Flags/pennants: ~85%+. These assume proper confirmation.

Head-and-Shoulders Bottoms (Inverse H&S): Three lows with middle lowest. Up breakout avg rise 30% bear, 41% bull. Failure rate 4% bear, 5% bull. Pullback 51% bear, 62% bull. Volume increases on breakout. Buy above neckline. Most reliable reversal. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

DOUBLE TOP — Two approximately equal highs with trough between. CONFIRMED only on break below trough (neckline) with increased volume. Tops should be 1+ month apart. Second top on LOWER volume = more likely genuine. TARGET: peaks-to-neckline distance projected down. Many apparent double tops are consolidation - wait for neckline break, do NOT anticipate.

Bump-and-Run Reversal Tops: Mirror of bottom. Price rises steeply in bump phase then reverses. Down breakout avg decline 27% bear, 33% bull. Failure rate 1% bear, 3% bull. Throwback 65% bear, 68% bull. Short after break below bump neckline. Best bear pattern #3 overall. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

SQUEEZE STRATEGY (TRIANGLE BREAKOUT): Identify converging trendlines on 15M chart forming a triangle/squeeze pattern. Wait for price to break out of the triangle with volume confirmation. Entry: on the breakout candle close above/below the trendline. Stop: opposite side of the triangle. Target: 2:1 R:R minimum. Best during kill zones when volume supports the breakout. The squeeze represents compression of volatility — when it releases, the move is explosive. Do NOT anticipate the direction — wait for the break. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

BULL FLAG — Sharp near-vertical rise (flagpole) then slight downward parallelogram on REDUCED volume. Breakout UPWARD on increased volume. TARGET: flagpole length projected from breakout. Duration 1-3 weeks. Among most reliable continuation patterns. Volume CONTRACT during flag, EXPAND on breakout.

Two-Bar Entry: Wait for counter-trend bar, then enter when next bar confirms trend resumption. Bull: bear bar then bull bar closing above bear bar high. Bear: bull bar then bear bar closing below bull bar low. Stop beyond confirmation bar.
BULL FLAG: Spike on heavy volume, tight parallel consolidation. Entry on break above flag resistance with volume. Stop below flag low. Tighter = more explosive.

BREAKOUT PREPARATION: Coulling volume dry-up + Grimes tight range + Edwards/Magee triangle/rectangle + Williams squeeze = all identify same pre-breakout condition. Low vol + tight range + alligator sleeping = energy building.

CUP AND HANDLE — Rounded bottom (U-shaped, not V) + small pullback (handle). Handle on RIGHT side, slight downward drift on LOW volume. BREAKOUT above handle resistance on heavy volume. Cup depth: 1/3 to 1/2 of prior advance. Handle retraces no more than 1/3-1/2 of cup depth. TARGET: cup depth projected upward.

Buy point: purchase at pivot point (10 cents above handle high) on breakout day with heavy volume. Volume should be at least 50% above average daily volume. If volume is weak the breakout will likely fail.

content: GAPS — FOUR TYPES: 1) Common: in congestion, quickly filled, no significance. 2) Breakaway: at pattern breakout, heavy volume, often NOT filled. 3) Runaway/Measuring: middle of trends, continuation. 4) Exhaustion: near end of trend, heavy volume, quickly filled. ISLAND REVERSAL: exhaustion + breakaway gap opposite = powerful reversal. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

STOCHASTICS — %K (fast) and %D (slow, 3-period SMA of %K). Default: 14-period %K, 3-period smoothing. OB >80, OS <20. SELL: %K crosses below %D above 80. BUY: %K crosses above %D below 20. Most effective in RANGING markets. In trends, can remain OB/OS. Filter: only trade signals in direction of larger trend.

High 2 (H2): Second pullback in bull trend. Two-legged pullback to moving average. More reliable than H1. Buy above second pullback bar's high. Often at trendline or MA support. Two legs = trapped bears from first pullback.
ETF SHORT: Below 200MA declining RS bottom sector groups. Cover above 10-day MA. No borrow fees.

MACD — 12-day EMA minus 26-day EMA = MACD line. 9-day EMA of MACD = Signal line. BUY: MACD crosses above Signal. SELL: crosses below. HISTOGRAM = MACD minus Signal - shows momentum acceleration. Divergence between histogram and price = early warning. Centerline crossover (MACD crossing zero) confirms trend change. CRYPTO: Consider faster params (8,17,9) for intraday crypto.

MACD HISTOGRAM (Pring) — Rising histogram = increasing bullish momentum. Falling = bearish. Histogram crossing zero = crossover imminent. TWO CONSECUTIVE declining peaks while price new high = most reliable momentum divergence. Histogram is LEADING indicator for MACD crossover.

RSI (Wilder, 14-period) — Range 0-100. Overbought >70, Oversold <30. Strong uptrend: RSI 40-80 (adjust OB to 80). Strong downtrend: RSI 20-60 (adjust OS to 20). DIVERGENCE: price new high + RSI lower high = top. Price new low + RSI higher low = bottom. FAILURE SWING: RSI crosses 70, pulls back, rallies fails 70 = sell.
LOSS AVERSION: 30-40% WR feels like failure. Dont look at individual P&L. Monthly only. CRYPTO: Use 80/20 thresholds instead of 70/30 due to momentum-driven nature.

ADX — Measures trend STRENGTH, not direction. 0-100. ADX >25 = trending. ADX <20 = ranging. ADX >40 = extreme trend (exhaustion possible). RISING ADX = trend strengthening. FALLING = weakening. +DI above -DI = bullish. -DI above +DI = bearish. ADX is the FILTER - use to SELECT strategy type.

content: BOLLINGER BANDS — Middle: 20-SMA. Upper: 20-SMA + 2 std dev. Lower: 20-SMA - 2 std dev. Bands CONTRACT in low volatility, EXPAND in high. Price riding upper band in strong uptrend is NOT bearish. SQUEEZE (narrowest in 6 months) precedes explosive move. %B = where price is relative to bands (0=lower, 1=upper). CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

BOLLINGER SQUEEZE STRATEGY — When BB contract inside Keltner Channels (BB width < KC width) = volatility squeeze. Entry: price breaks out of squeeze (BB expand outside KC). Direction = breakout direction. Stop: opposite band or recent swing. Catches beginning of major moves.

OBV (On-Balance Volume) — Cumulative: add volume on up days, subtract on down days. OBV must CONFIRM price trend. Bearish divergence: price new high, OBV fails = distribution. Bullish divergence: price new low, OBV holds = accumulation. OBV often LEADS price - breakout in OBV before price signals coming direction.

VOLUME — FOUNDATIONAL: Volume expands in direction of prevailing trend. Uptrend: volume up on rallies, down on pullbacks. Downtrend: volume up on declines, down on bounces. VOLUME PRECEDES PRICE: heavy volume at bottoms = accumulation. Drying volume at tops = distribution. Climactic volume + rapid price change = climax = reversal.

FIBONACCI RETRACEMENTS — Key levels: 38.2%, 50%, 61.8%. After significant move, expect retracement to one level. 38.2% = shallow (strong trend). 50% = normal (most common). 61.8% = deep (golden ratio). Retracement >61.8% = trend may be over. ENTRY: buy 38.2-61.8% zone, stop below 61.8%.

PRICE TARGETS CONFLUENCE — 1) Measured move (pattern height from breakout). 2) Fibonacci extension (161.8%). 3) Prior S/R levels. 4) Round numbers. 3+ methods at same price = high-confidence target. Partial profits at first target, trail stop. Define targets BEFORE entry.

ELLIOTT WAVE — IMPULSE RULES: Wave 2 NEVER >100% of wave 1. Wave 3 NEVER shortest impulse wave. Wave 4 NEVER enters wave 1 territory (except diagonals). CORRECTIVE: 3-wave A-B-C. 5-3 pattern repeats at every degree (fractal). Corrective waves harder to predict than impulse waves. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

HAMMER and HANGING MAN — Small real body at TOP + long lower shadow (2-3x body). Hammer at BOTTOM of downtrend = bullish reversal. Hanging Man at TOP of uptrend = bearish reversal. CONFIRMATION REQUIRED: next candle must confirm direction. Without confirmation, just potential signals - do NOT trade in isolation.

Dark Cloud Cover: After uptrend, opens above prior high then closes deeply into prior white body. Requires >50% penetration for significance. Greater penetration = more bearish. Bearish engulfing is stronger version. Use high as resistance. Heavy volume on open = blow-off.

Candle Patterns as Support/Resistance: Use highs of bearish engulfing/dark-cloud cover as resistance. Use lows of bullish engulfing/piercing pattern as support. Cluster of candle signals at same level = very strong S/R. Wait for bounce off these levels.

Nison Volume Confirmation: Heavy volume on engulfing pattern increases significance. Volume surge on third candle of morning/evening star confirms. Blow-off volume at top (high volume with no price progress). Declining volume with price rise = bearish divergence.

MORNING/EVENING STAR — Three-candle reversals. Morning Star (bullish): 1) Long bearish candle. 2) Small-bodied star gapping down. 3) Long bullish closing above midpoint of first. Evening Star (bearish): mirror at top. Heavy volume on 1st and 3rd candles. Doji star variant = even stronger signal.

Harami Cross: Harami where second candle is a doji. Stronger signal than regular harami. After uptrend, very bearish (like harami but more decisive). After downtrend, very bullish. Major reversal signal. Requires confirmation.

CANDLESTICK FAILURE — Failed patterns MORE powerful than successful. Hammer FAILS (below low) = very bearish. Morning star FAILS = continuation. Bearish engulfing FAILS (above high) = strong bullish. FAILURE traps wrong-side traders, fueling opposite move. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

INTERMARKET ANALYSIS — 1) USD and Commodities: INVERSELY correlated. 2) Commodities and Bonds: INVERSELY correlated (rising commodities = inflation = lower bond prices). 3) Bonds and Stocks: POSITIVELY correlated long-term. 4) Dollar and Stocks: complex, generally positive short-term. RELATIONSHIPS BREAK DOWN during crises.

A/D LINE (Advance/Decline) — Cumulative daily advances minus declines. Must CONFIRM new market highs. Index new high + A/D line NOT = BEARISH divergence (narrowing leadership). A/D line new high BEFORE index = bullish. Leading indicator of major tops and bottoms.
VOLUME DOCTRINE (E/M): Without volume = SUSPECT. 1) Breakouts = expanding. 2) Reversals = climax. 3) Continuations = declining in consolidation. 4) Volume confirms DIRECTION.

Margin of safety: never buy a security unless price is 25-30% below conservatively calculated intrinsic value. This buffer protects against analytical errors, bad luck, and market volatility. The central concept of investing. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.
VALUATION: Price vs value. Intrinsic=DCF. Buy 25-30% below. Most crypto = pure speculation.

Opening Reversal: Strong move in first 15-30 min that reverses. After gap up: if price can't make new high, potential opening reversal down. After gap down: if can't make new low, opening reversal up. Fade the opening move.
TREND ID: Higher highs/lows. 20 EMA filter. ADX>25. 50/200 golden cross = major bull.

ATR (Average True Range) — True Range = max(High-Low, |High-Prior Close|, |Low-Prior Close|). ATR(14) standard. Uses: 1) Position sizing: risk / ATR = shares. 2) Trailing stop: Close - 2xATR (long). 3) Volatility filter: trade breakouts when ATR expanding. ATR CONTRACTION precedes expansion.

VOLATILITY REGIME — ATR(14) as % of price: <1% = low vol (mean reversion). 1-3% = normal (trend-following). >3% = high vol (wider stops, smaller positions). ATR percentile rank over 252 days: <20th = compression (breakout imminent). >80th = expansion (exhaustion possible).

POSITION SIZING — Shares = (Account Risk $) / (Entry - Stop). Account Risk = Account x Risk% (1-2%). Example: $100K, 1% = $1000 risk. Entry $50, Stop $48 = $2 risk. Shares = 500. Ensures CONSISTENT risk across all trades regardless of price or volatility.

VIX — CBOE Volatility Index (30-day implied vol). VIX >30 = high fear = potential bottom. VIX <15 = complacency = top forming. Spike >40 = extreme panic = tradeable bottom. VIX compression (<12) = volatility expansion coming. Inverse to S&P 500.

SENTIMENT CONTRARIAN — Majority bullish = near top. Bearish = near bottom. AAII: >60% bulls = caution, >60% bears = opportunity. P/C >1.0 = fear = buy. VIX >30 = fear. <15 = complacency. COT: commercials at extremes. Use as timing tool, not primary signal.
S&P CORRELATIONS: DAX 0.85+, Euro Stoxx 0.90+, VIX -0.75. Euro Stoxx leads S&P 3-5 min European hours.
VIX-STOCK: -0.75. <15=complacency. >30=fear. Contango=bullish. Backwardation=bearish. Flip=defend.
BE FEARFUL WHEN GREEDY: VIX<15+highs=cautious. VIX>40+crashes=aggressive. Deployed billions 2008.

FIBONACCI CLUSTER ZONES — Calculate multiple Fib measurements from DIFFERENT swing points. Where 38.2%, 50%, 61.8% from different swings converge = extremely strong S/R. More measurements converging = higher probability. Use 3+ swing points per asset.

RSI + MACD DIVERGENCE CONFIRMATION — RSI divergence AND MACD histogram simultaneous divergence = extremely high probability reversal. BOTH must diverge together. Third: volume declining. Triple divergence marks major tops/bottoms. Wait for trendline break before entry.

TREND REVERSAL CHECKLIST — 1) Trendline broken (close beyond). 2) Prior swing point violated. 3) MA crossover (50/200). 4) Volume confirmation. 5) Momentum divergence (RSI/MACD). 6) Breadth divergence (A/D line). Minimum 3 for high-confidence reversal.

MOMENTUM DIVERGENCE TIMING — Divergence can persist weeks/months. DO NOT trade on divergence alone. Wait for: 1) Trendline break. 2) MA crossover. 3) Momentum zero-line break. 4) S/R break. Need 2 of 4 confirmations after divergence. Divergence = WARNING, not trigger.
SECTOR ROTATION: Early=XLF,XLY. Mid=XLK,XLI. Late=XLE,XLB. Recession=XLU,XLP,XLV. Monthly RS. Leads price 1-3mo.

MULTIPLE TIMEFRAME ANALYSIS — Rule of 4-6 between timeframes. LONGER timeframe = trend to trade. INTERMEDIATE = signal. SHORTER = precise entry. Example: Weekly uptrend + Daily pullback to support + Intraday reversal = high-probability long. Never trade against larger timeframe.
MULTIPLE TIMEFRAME STRUCTURE: Higher TF sets context (trend/range). Trade TF provides setups. Lower TF provides entry precision. Disagreement between TFs = reduce size or skip.
YIELD CURVE: 2Y>10Y=inversion=recession 6-18mo. Steep=expansion. Monitor 2s10s. Negative=defensive.

STOP-LOSS METHODOLOGY — 1) Pattern-based: below pattern low. 2) ATR: entry - 2xATR(14). 3) Percentage: varies by volatility. 4) MA-based: below key MA. 5) Volatility-adjusted: wider in high vol. ATR stop most adaptive. Stop at LOGICAL invalidation point, not arbitrary $.

False Breakout Trade: Price breaks level then immediately reverses. Enter counter-breakout. Stop beyond false break extreme. Target: opposite side of range. False breaks are more profitable than real breakouts for scalpers.
ETF SIZING: Max 10-15% per ETF. Leveraged 3-5%. ATR stops 2x 20-day. Max 30% one sector. Cash 30-50% when unclear.
TURTLE SIZING: ATR normalized. Unit=(Account x 1%)/(N x $/pt). Max 4/market, 10/direction, 12 total.

OSCILLATOR SIGNALS IN RANGES — When ADX <20 (no trend). Buy: RSI <30 + Stochastic <20 + price at support. Sell: RSI >70 + Stochastic >80 + price at resistance. Multiple confirmation dramatically improves. MA crossovers produce WHIPSAWS in ranges - avoid.

LEADING vs LAGGING INDICATORS — Leading: oscillators (RSI, ROC, Stochastic) - signal BEFORE price, prone to false signals. Lagging: trend-following (MA, MACD, SAR) - signal AFTER change, more reliable. Use BOTH: leading for early warning, lagging for confirmation.

THREE LEVELS OF ANALYSIS: 1) LAGGING — indicators (MACD, RSI, Stochastic) — derivatives of price, always follow. 2) REAL-TIME — price action — watching what's happening as an effect. 3) LEADING — volume — watching the narrative BEFORE price creates it. Volume is the only leading indicator. CVD moves before price. Aggression appears before the breakout. If you're using lagging indicators, you're always behind.
PROBABILITY FRAMEWORK: Base probability per setup. +5-10% each for: volume confirmation, MTF alignment, key S/R location. -10% each for: counter-trend, extended, conflicting. >70% = full size.
ZWEIG BREADTH THRUST - 10-day ratio of advances/(advances+declines). >0.615 = thrust. Only 14 times since 1945. 100% of occurrences: market higher 12 months later. Average +25%. Extremely bullish.

TRIANGLE BREAKOUT RULES — Breakout between 1/2 and 3/4 of width for reliability. First 1/2 = premature. After 3/4 = less powerful. Volume MUST expand significantly. Low volume breakout = likely false. Throwback/pullback ~60% within 1-2 weeks = SECONDARY entry.
ETF ENTRY: Pullback to 10/20 EMA bounce. Or flag/pennant breakout. Never chase 5%+ from support.

MOMENTUM CONCEPTS — Momentum = rate of price change. Zero-line crossover = trend change. MOMENTUM LEADS PRICE - peaks/troughs before price. Longer lookback = more significant but delayed. Divergence = MOST important signal - warns of exhaustion before reversal.

OSCILLATOR BOUNDS IN TRENDS — Strong uptrend: oscillators spend more time OB (RSI 50-80). Strong downtrend: more time OS (RSI 20-60). Adjust thresholds: uptrend OB = 80, OS = 40. Downtrend OB = 60, OS = 20. Fixed 70/30 in strong trends = premature signals.

BREAKOUT VALIDATION — 1) Close beyond S/R (not just intraday). 2) Volume 50%+ above 50-day avg. 3) Follow-through next 1-2 bars. 4) Retest holds. 5) Multiple timeframes confirm. All 5 = ~90% success. 1-2 only = ~50%. Volume is SINGLE most important factor. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

PRING HIERARCHY — 1) Monthly KST/Special K for primary. 2) Monthly MACD for cycles. 3) Monthly RSI for OB/OS. 4) Weekly momentum for intermediate. 5) Daily for timing. START with monthly, work down. Never take daily signal against monthly momentum.

MULTIPLE INDICATOR STRATEGY (Pring) — Use indicators from DIFFERENT categories: 1) Trend-following (MA, MACD). 2) Momentum (RSI, ROC). 3) Volume (OBV). 4) Breadth (A/D). At least one from each must confirm. Two from same category = less confirmation than one from each.

MULTI-TIMEFRAME ALIGNMENT - Daily RSI buy + weekly ROC positive + monthly KST above signal = MAXIMUM probability. Each timeframe adds conviction. Entry only when all align. Partial alignment = partial position.
RSI TRENDS: Uptrend 40-80 (40=oversold). Downtrend 20-60 (60=overbought). 30/70 only for ranging.

SWING STRUCTURE + VOLUME: Grimes swing analysis + Coulling volume analysis. Impulse legs should show expanding volume. Corrective legs contracting. If volume contradicts swing structure, trust VOLUME over price.

EDGE QUANTIFICATION: Each book provides different edge components. Volume edge (Coulling). Pattern edge (Edwards/Magee). Momentum edge (Williams). Structural edge (Grimes). Sentiment edge (Bernstein). Stack edges.

SMART MONEY DETECTION FRAMEWORK: Coulling VPA (volume-price divergence) + Bernstein COT (hedger positioning) + Wyckoff concepts (accumulation/distribution). Three independent methods to detect institutional activity.
RISING MARKET FALLING VOLUME: Higher highs on progressively lower vol = distribution disguised as uptrend. SM selling each rally. Volume trend reversed. Do NOT add to longs.
MARKET DIRECTION: 3-5 distribution days in 2-3wk = top. Follow-through 1.5%+ heavier volume = confirmed uptrend.

Flags: Small rectangular consolidation against prevailing trend, sloping opposite. Down breakout performance measures to trend low. Failure rate 0% bear, 1% bull. Throwback 40% bear. Brief consolidation of 1-3 weeks. Trade in direction of prior trend.

Bulkowski Throwback/Pullback Stats: Throwbacks occur in 62% of bull breakouts. Pullbacks occur in 54% of bear breakouts. Average throwback retraces 48% of breakout move. Takes 10-15 days. After throwback, price resumes breakout direction 67% of time.

Bulkowski Throwback Strategy: After bullish breakout, 62% chance of throwback. If throwback occurs, price averages 6% lower but resumes. Buy the throwback at breakout level. Better entry than chasing breakout. Stop below throwback low.

PULLBACK ENTRY: After initial morning spike (first 5-15 min), wait for pullback to VWAP or 9 EMA. Enter when price bounces off VWAP with increasing volume. Stop below VWAP or recent swing low. Target previous high or breakout level. DON'T CHASE THE SPIKE. Only trade FIRST pullback — second/third have lower probability. First pullback is where institutions reload.

Support and Resistance Zones: Not exact price but zone where price reacts. Use prior swing highs/lows, round numbers, moving averages. Zone width depends on timeframe. More touches = stronger zone. Look for reaction candles at zones.

MOMENTUM DIVERGENCE DEPTH — Bearish: 2-3 higher price highs while momentum lower highs. Each successive divergence MORE significant. First = warning. Second = high probability. Third = almost certain reversal. Wait for trendline break as trigger.

MOMENTUM AND TREND CONFIRMATION — New price high/low MUST be confirmed by new momentum high/low. Price new high + momentum NOT = divergence = warning. Price new high + momentum new high = confirmed, continue. Absence of confirmation = reduce exposure.
REGIME: RISK-ON=stocks/bonds/commodities/USD. RISK-OFF=opposite. STAGFLATION=stocks+down/commodities up. GOLDILOCKS=stocks up/bonds flat.

SELLING CLIMAX: 1) Extreme volume spike. 2) Wide spread down. 3) Panic/capitulation. 4) Followed by narrow spread or reversal. Smart money ACCUMULATING from panicked sellers. Transfer from weak to strong hands.

Rising Windows (Bullish Gap): Gap between prior high and current low. Continuation signal - go with window direction. Entire window becomes support. Stop: close below window bottom. Multiple windows: trend intact until last window closed.
S = Supply and demand: daily trading volume should increase significantly on up days and decrease on down days. Big volume demand at key price points signals institutional accumulation.

Identify market regime before applying strategy. Trend-following works in trending markets. Mean-reversion works in ranging markets. Applying wrong strategy to wrong regime is the most common systematic error.

VOLUME PROFILE + VSA INTEGRATION: Use volume profile to identify key S/R zones (HVN/LVN). Use VSA within those zones to detect smart money activity. Volume profile provides LOCATION. VSA provides INTENT. Together they reveal WHERE and WHEN.

VOLUME PROFILE CONCEPTS: LVN = lack of volume = price continues through. Good reaction level. Value area = 70% of volume. POC = highest volume level. Market seeks balance → target POC and value area, not extremes. Out of balance = searching for new balance. Profile tells you WHERE you are before applying any strategy. Without profile = caging an animal not made for a cage. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.
FAILURES AS SIGNALS - Failed patterns produce stronger moves than successful. Trapped traders create forced buying/selling. Always identify what constitutes FAILURE of your setup - trade the failure.

Brooks on Failed Moves: Failed moves lead to fast moves in opposite direction. When trapped traders exit en masse, momentum accelerates. Trade the failure, not the breakout. Most breakout attempts fail.

Final Flag: Last consolidation pattern before reversal. After strong trend, flag forms but breaks opposite direction. Trapped trend traders create momentum for reversal. Enter counter-trend on flag breakout failure.

Broadening Bottoms: Price swings widen with lower lows and higher highs forming megaphone shape. Up breakout avg rise 33%, failure rate 13% in bull market. Down breakout avg decline 18%, failure rate 9%. Throwback/pullback 64%. Volume decreases 67% of time. Trade the breakout direction with stop inside pattern.
MARKET STATES: 1) TRENDING: impulsive legs, overlap < 50%. 2) TIGHT RANGE: narrow, overlapping. 3) LOOSE RANGE: wider swings. 4) BROADENING: expanding. Each requires DIFFERENT strategy.

FAIR VALUE GAP (FVG) — Bullish: candle[i-2].high < candle[i].low. Rapid upward movement creating supply/demand imbalance. Market often returns to fill gaps before continuing. ONLY trade unfilled FVGs. When price revisits and closes through → mark as filled, invalid. Best FVGs aligned with higher timeframe trend direction. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

FVG — Bearish: candle[i-2].low > candle[i].high. Rapid downward movement. Price retraces back into gap before continuing down. Use as short entry zones when aligned with bearish higher TF bias. FVGs at round numbers or previous structure = higher probability. BPR (Balanced Price Range) = overlapping FVGs = very strong level.

ENTRY REFINEMENT: After identifying bias (4h) and setup zone (15m FVG/OB), drop to 5m for entry. On 5m: 1) Price reaches setup zone. 2) BOS in trade direction on 5m. 3) Pullback to 5m FVG or OB within the 15m zone. 4) Enter on pullback with stop below 5m structure. Tight stop (10-20 pips), not 50-100. If stop too wide = setup not refined enough.
MA CONFLUENCE: 10/20/50 convergence = powerful move. Compression after consolidation.
ABCD PATTERN: Impulse A-B, pullback to C (38-50% Fib), continuation D. Entry at C with MA/VWAP support. Stop below C. Target = AB leg extension.
VWAP: Price above = bullish, below = bearish. Buy pullbacks to VWAP in uptrend. Acts as magnet and S/R. Institutional execution benchmark.

Bulkowski Volume Pattern: Volume should be higher on breakout day than prior 50-day average. 59% of breakouts have above-avg volume. Declining volume during pattern formation is bullish for breakouts. Volume spike on breakout confirms.
INTERMARKET DIVERGENCE: Gold up miners not=catch up. S&P up credit widening=correct. Divergences signal stress.

Bulkowski For Best Performance Rules: Patterns with lowest failure rates perform best. Look for heavy breakout volume (above 50-day avg). Throwbacks/pullbacks hurt performance by 6-8%. Tall patterns perform better than short. Wider price swings within pattern = stronger move. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

Bulkowski Bull vs Bear Identification: Compare pattern failure rates. Bull market patterns: lower failure rates on up breakouts. Bear market: lower failure rates on down breakouts. Match trade direction to market regime.
Continuation/Runaway Gaps: Gap mid-trend, confirming strong momentum. Does not fill quickly. Can act as midpoint for measured move. Trade with trend. Volume should be above average.

Two Legs: Most corrections and trends have two legs. After first leg, expect correction then second leg. Two-legged pullbacks are most reliable entries. Count legs by measuring moves away from MA. Each leg often equals measured move.

Measured Moves: Price often moves equal distances. First leg height = second leg target. Breakout gap or initial impulse = measured move target. Used for profit targets. Works for both pullbacks and trend continuations.

Bulkowski Dead-Cat Bounce Mechanics: Stock drops 20%+ in one session. Bounces 20-40% of decline over 4-8 days. Then resumes decline. Short into bounce strength. Cover when bounce fades. Don't bottom fish. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

Marubozu: Long real body with no or very tiny shadows. White marubozu (bullish): opens at low, closes at high. Black marubozu (bearish): opens at high, closes at low. Shows total domination by one side. Strong continuation or reversal depending on context.

Proper Break: Convincing break with strong candle body closing beyond level. Body > 75% of candle range. Follow-through on next candle. Volume confirmation. Trade with the break. Wait for pullback to broken level for entry.

Two-Bar Reversal: Two bars that together form reversal. Bull: bear bar followed by bull bar that closes above both opens. Bear: bull bar followed by bear bar closing below both opens. Shows rapid shift in control. Entry on break of second bar.

Climax Bars: Extreme trend bars at end of extended move. Bull climax: huge bull bar after long rally. Bear climax: huge bear bar after long decline. Often followed by reversal or trading range. Don't chase climax bars. Wait for pullback.

Spike and Channel Bear: Strong down bar(s) followed by bear channel. Short rallies to top of channel. Don't buy the spike low (premature). Wait for channel to form, then sell rallies. Breakout above channel = trend change.

Magnet Levels: Price tends to gravitate to key levels. Prior swing highs/lows, round numbers, opening price, prior day high/low. Price moves toward magnets. Once reached, price may reverse. Use for profit targets.

Trading Range as Magnet: Price in trading range is attracted to opposite side. If at top of range, expect move to bottom and vice versa. Breakout from range in trend direction. Failed breakout = strong reversal to opposite side.

Magnet of Prior Day Close: Price gravitates to prior day's close, especially on gap opens. Gap fills happen because prior close is a magnet. Trade the fill then reassess. If gap doesn't fill in 3 bars, the gap direction is strong.

Candle Body vs Shadow Analysis: Body shows committed buying/selling. Shadow shows rejected price. Long shadow + small body = rejection. Long body + small shadow = commitment. Use relative proportions for bias.

Volman on Momentum: Strong momentum candle = don't fade it. Wait for momentum to exhaust (small bodies, shadows). Enter fade only after momentum dies. Momentum candles with body > 75% range = wait. Don't catch falling knives.

ASIAN SESSION RANGE: Asian session (7 PM - 2 AM EST) typically forms a tight range. This range is CRITICAL for London session trading. Mark Asian high and low. During London open: price often sweeps one side of the Asian range, traps traders, then reverses. The sweep of Asian high/low during London kill zone is one of the most reliable setups in forex/crypto. Entry on the reversal candle after the sweep. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

Drill-Down Entry Checklist: (1) Higher TF direction established. (2) 5min pulls back to S/R zone. (3) Rejection candle forms at zone. (4) Entry on break of rejection candle. (5) Stop beyond zone. (6) Target next S/R. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

Volman Market Phase: Trending (trade pullbacks), Ranging (fade extremes), Transitional (uncertain - reduce size). Identify phase from recent swing structure. Reclassify every 30 min. Each phase requires different strategy.

HIGH VOLUME + WIDE SPREAD UP = genuine buying pressure confirmed. Smart money is driving price higher AND retail is following the momentum. This is trend continuation confirmed. These candles appear at breakouts and during impulse legs. Do NOT fade this signal under any circumstances.
VSA THREE DIMENSIONS: 1) Volume (effort). 2) Spread (result). 3) Close position. Close at HIGH + high vol = demand. Close at LOW + high vol = supply. Close at MIDDLE + high vol = ABSORPTION.

SHAKEOUT/SPRING: Wide spread DOWN candle that pierces below established support then reverses. Designed to trigger retail stop-losses below support. If volume is LOW this is a spring (accumulation trap). If volume is HIGH this may be a genuine breakdown. Price must RECOVER above support quickly to confirm. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

HIGH VOLUME + NARROW SPREAD = ABSORPTION. Smart money buying heavily while capping price. ACCUMULATING without letting price rise. At tops: distributing without letting price fall. Large player working orders.

LOW VOLUME + WIDE SPREAD = complete lack of genuine market interest. The move looks impressive on the chart but volume data says nobody participated. At breakouts: low volume breakout is fake and will reverse. In trends: widening spread on declining volume = trend exhaustion is imminent. This is a TRAP. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

NO SUPPLY BAR: Bearish candle with VERY LOW volume and NARROW spread appearing in a downtrend. Sellers have completely EXHAUSTED. No willing sellers remain at current prices. This is extremely bullish when seen at support after a prolonged downtrend. Wait for confirmation candle before entering long.

UPTHRUST AFTER DISTRIBUTION (UTAD): An upthrust occurring at the TOP of a trading range after prolonged distribution phase. Price spikes above range resistance to trap late buyers, then reverses sharply back inside. This is the FINAL TRAP before markdown begins. Short entry on reversal candle closing back inside range.
BREAKOUT VOLUME: Genuine = 2-3x average volume expanding. Fake = low/declining. After breakout volume remains elevated on continuation, contracts on pullbacks. Volume surge on pullback = failing.
SMART MONEY ACCUMULATION: Downtrend, volume spike (climax), rally, return to lows on MUCH LOWER vol, narrowing range. Each low on lower vol = fewer sellers. SM absorbing supply.
GAP TRADING: Earnings gaps 3x+ volume continue. Gaps below support keep falling. Fill in 1hr = fade. No fill = trend with it.
END-OF-DAY FADE: New highs last 30 min declining volume = gap down next day. Selloff into close low volume = gap up.

5 PILLARS STOCK SELECTION: 1) Pre-market gappers (>4% gap up). 2) Catalyst (news, earnings, FDA). 3) Float (<100M shares, ideally <20M). 4) Volume (>500K pre-market, >1M by open). 5) Price ($2-$20 range). ALL 5 required. Missing one reduces probability significantly. Gap + catalyst + low float = highest probability. Low float + high volume = explosive moves because limited supply meets high demand. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.
AO ZERO-LINE CROSS: Negative to positive = buy. Positive to negative = sell. MOMENTUM SHIFT. Never trade against AO zero-line cross. Wait for candle close to confirm.

FRACTAL THEORY: Markets are fundamentally FRACTAL in nature meaning the same patterns repeat at every time scale. A 5-minute chart looks like a daily chart which looks like a weekly chart. Use MULTIPLE timeframes for comprehensive trade analysis. High fractal dimension indicates choppy ranging conditions. Low fractal dimension indicates trending.
FRACTAL SIGNAL: UP fractal = bar high HIGHER than two bars each side (5-bar). DOWN fractal = bar low LOWER than neighbors. Broken = continuation. Unbroken = potential reversal.
THIRD WISE MAN: Wait for BUY fractal, enter when price breaks ABOVE fractal high. AO must be positive. Stop below fractal low. Fractal breakout = TRIGGER, AO = FILTER.
ALLIGATOR: Jaw (13 SMMA offset 8, blue, slow). Teeth (8 SMMA offset 5, red). Lips (5 SMMA offset 3, green, fast). TWISTED = sleeping (range). DIVERGE = eating (trend).
ALLIGATOR RULES: LIPS crossing ABOVE Teeth+Jaw = BUY. BELOW = SELL. Lips cross back = take profits. NEVER trade when lines intertwined (sleeping = chop).
MFI WILLIAMS: (High-Low)/Volume. GREEN (MFI+ Vol+) = continuation. FADE (MFI- Vol-) = pausing. FAKE (MFI+ Vol-) = false move. SQUAT (MFI- Vol+) = battle, breakout imminent.
MFI SQUAT: MFI declining + Volume increasing. Price per vol SHRINKING despite heavy participation. Battle in progress. Breakout IMMINENT. Wait then trade aggressively.

VOLUME AS LEADING INDICATOR: Coulling emphasizes volume leads price. Schabacker noted same. Edwards/Magee require volume confirmation. Williams MFI measures volume quality. All four books treat volume as primary, not secondary.
PROFITUNITY SYSTEM: 1) Alligator state. 2) First Wise Man signal. 3) AO momentum. 4) Fractals for breakout. 5) MFI quality filter. Never trade sleeping. Only eating direction.
FIVE DIMENSIONS: 1) Fractal (motive). 2) AO (acceleration). 3) Alligator (balance). 4) MFI (facilitation). 5) Direction. ALL five required for complete trade.

MULTI-INDICATOR CONVERGENCE: Williams five dimensions + Coulling volume analysis + Edwards/Magee pattern recognition. When multiple independent systems agree = statistical edge maximized. Trade these convergences.
TIGHT PATTERNS: 3-4wk closes within 1-2%. Institutional support. Breakout from tight = explosive.
SIGNAL BAR vs ENTRY BAR: Signal = setup (reversal candle at S/R, narrow in pullback). Entry = trigger (closes above signal high). Good signal: close near extreme, reasonable range.
POSITION SIZING (GRIMES): Risk 1-2% max per trade. Size = Account Risk / (Entry-Stop). High prob = 2%. Lower = 0.5-1%. NEVER exceed 2%. Size is PRIMARY risk control.

Double Pressure: When higher timeframe and lower timeframe align in same direction. E.g., 60min bullish + 5min bullish = strongest signal. Wait for alignment before entry. Reduces false signals significantly. Key concept for scalpers. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.
L = Leader or laggard: use Relative Strength (RS) rating to identify leaders. Buy stocks with RS 80 or above (top 20% of performers). Avoid laggards with low RS. Industry group strength matters too.
PRE-MARKET: 6:00 news. 6:30 gappers. 7:00 S/R. 8:00 direction. 8:30 data. 9:15 plan. Prep=80% success.

THREE-STEP MODEL (Trend Following — NY Session): Step 1 MARKET STATE: only trade when OUT of balance. In balance = stay out. Step 2 LOCATION: swing points + low volume nodes. Set alert BELOW LVN, don't place limit orders. Wait to SEE aggression first. Step 3 EXECUTION: big aggressive orders (bubbles). Seller aggression → short. Stop ABOVE aggression candle. Target: previous balance area / POC. FULL exit at target — 70% reversal probability. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

CHANGE OF CHARACTER (CHoCH): First break AGAINST the trend = potential reversal. Uptrend CHoCH = price breaks below most recent swing low. Downtrend CHoCH = price breaks above most recent swing high. CHoCH is a WARNING — tighten stops or exit. Do NOT counter-trend trade without CHoCH confirmation. After CHoCH, wait for pullback to broken level for entry in new direction.

SMT DIVERGENCE (Smart Money Tool): Compare correlated indices (e.g., BTC vs ETH, or BTC vs SOL) for divergent behavior. When one makes a new high but the other fails to confirm = divergence signal. Bullish SMT: BTC makes lower low but ETH makes higher low = ETH showing relative strength, expect reversal up. Bearish SMT: BTC makes higher high but ETH fails to confirm = weakness, expect reversal down. SMT divergence at key levels = high-probability reversal signal. This works because smart money moves differently across correlated assets.

VOLUME CONFIRMATION OF BREAKOUTS — Breakout WITHOUT volume increase is SUSPECT. Valid breakout: volume 50%+ above average, ideally 100%+. Low-volume breakouts = false breakouts that reverse. Larger pattern = more volume needed. Volume is SINGLE most important breakout confirmation factor.

High-Price Gapping Play: Small real bodies near highs after strong move, then gap up. Bullish continuation. The consolidation relieved overbought. Buy above the window. Voided if market closes below window.

Trend Channel Lines: Parallel to trendline on opposite side. Connect swing highs in uptrend, swing lows in downtrend. Price reaching channel line = potential reversal. Overshoot of channel line = climax. Touch of channel line = profit target.
Bullish Body Characteristics: Opens near low, closes near high. Body > 60% of total range. Little/no upper shadow. Growing body size vs prior candles. All show buying pressure dominance.
Intraday Bias: Determine from first 30-60 min price action. Higher highs and lows = bullish bias. Lower highs and lows = bearish bias. Flat = neutral/scalp both sides. Adjust bias as day develops.

Candle Sequence Bias: 3+ consecutive bull bodies = bullish. 3+ consecutive bear bodies = bearish. Alternating colors = ranging. Growing bodies = strengthening trend. Shrinking bodies = trend weakening.

Close Position Bias: Where price closes relative to range determines bias. Close in upper third = bullish. Close in lower third = bearish. Close in middle third = neutral. Series of closes in upper third confirms uptrend.

Drill-Down Approach: Use higher timeframe for bias, lower for entry. 60min for direction, 5min for timing. Only trade when both align. Higher TF support + 5min reversal = high probability entry. This is Volman's core methodology.
Breakeven Stop: Move stop to entry after price moves 1x risk in your favor. Protects capital. Allows free trade. Don't move too early (gets stopped out). 1:1 risk-reward minimum before adjusting.
Partial Profit Taking: Take 50% off at 1:1 risk-reward. Let remainder run with trailing stop. Reduces emotional pressure. Ensures winner even if reversal. Standard for scalpers.

Bulkowski Partial Declines/Rises: Partial decline in bottom pattern = bullish. Price comes down but stays above pattern low then breaks out up. Partial rise in top pattern = bearish. Price rises but stays below pattern high then breaks down.

Price Action Principle: Price is the ultimate indicator. Everything you need is in the bars. No lagging indicators needed. The market speaks through price. Learn to listen by studying bar-by-bar action.

Volman Trade Review: Review every trade for quality of setup, entry, management. Categorize as A, B, or C setup. Only take A setups when starting. Track win rate by setup quality. Improvement comes from review.

Pattern Trading Execution: (1) Identify pattern forming. (2) Wait for breakout. (3) Confirm with volume. (4) Enter on breakout close. (5) Stop inside pattern. (6) Target: pattern height projected from breakout.

Brooks Trending vs Ranging: If consecutive bars overlap significantly = range. If bars make new highs with little overlap = trend. In trend: buy pullbacks. In range: fade extremes. Most important question in trading. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

Brooks Always in Long/Short: After strong bull breakout, traders are 'always in long'. After strong bear breakdown, 'always in short'. Stay with position until opposite signal. Don't exit winners prematurely. Let the market tell you when to exit.
SUPPORT/RESISTANCE FOUNDATIONS: Support = concentration of buying orders at a price. Resistance = concentration of selling. Created by prior trading activity at those levels. Memory of price levels.

PATTERN + VOLUME SEQUENCE: Edwards/Magee pattern definitions + Coulling volume rules = complete pattern trading system. Every pattern has specific volume requirements for confirmation. Without volume = pattern suspect.

UNIVERSAL TRUTH: Every book confirms - volume is the most important secondary indicator. Price is primary. Volume confirms or denies price action. When price and volume disagree, trust VOLUME. This is non-negotiable.
Four moat types: (1) intangible assets brands patents licenses (2) switching costs (3) network effects (4) cost advantages from process location scale. Evaluate moat durability over 10+ years.
Look for consistently high ROE with low debt. High ROE with little leverage indicates genuine competitive advantage. High ROE with high leverage is a red flag masquerading as quality.

5-Step Process: (1) Set clear goals (2) Identify problems preventing goals (3) Diagnose root causes (4) Design solutions (5) Execute. Iterative process applies to trading investing and all of life. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.
Everything cycles. Economies cycle between expansion and contraction. Markets cycle between greed and fear. Credit cycles drive most movements. Know where you are in the cycle to position correctly.
Economic machine: (1) productivity growth trend (2) long-term debt cycle 50-75 yrs (3) short-term debt cycle 5-8 yrs. Understanding position in each cycle guides optimal asset allocation.
Absolute momentum (time-series): asset has positive momentum if current price above price N months ago (typically 12). Only go long assets in uptrends. Shift to cash/bonds in downtrends.

Relative momentum (cross-sectional): rank assets by trailing returns and buy strongest performers. Winners tend to keep winning over 3-12 month horizons. Exploit continuation of trends. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

M = Market direction: determine the general market trend before making any purchases. 3 out of 4 stocks follow the market direction. Do not fight the trend. Buy in confirmed uptrends sell in downtrends.
Reversion to the mean: today star performers are tomorrow laggards. Hot funds cool off. Hot sectors revert. The market is brutally efficient at eliminating excess returns over time.
Return on Capital = EBIT / (Net Working Capital + Net Fixed Assets). Measures how efficiently a business generates profits from its capital. Higher is better. Look for 25%+ ROC.
Good companies at bargain prices: the magic formula combines quality (ROC) with cheapness (EY). Buying cheap bad companies or expensive good companies both underperform. You need both.
BULL CALL VERTICAL: Buy lower call, sell higher. Limited risk/profit. Better than naked call - reduces cost.
ETF ROTATION MODEL: Rank sectors by 3mo RS monthly. Long top 3. Rebalance monthly. Outperformed 3-5% annually.
REGIME: SPY>200+50>200=bull long only. <200+50<200=bear short/cash. Mixed=reduce 50%.
GLOBAL LEADERSHIP: US leads 1-5 days. Europe 1-3. Asia 1-2. DAX up 2%=S&P gaps up. Early warning.
MUNGER INVERSION: How to lose not win. Avoid leverage, bad businesses, overpaying, crowd-following.
TURTLE ENTRY: Sys1: 20-day breakout exit 10-day. Skip if last won. Sys2: 55-day. Take all. 70% profit from 5-10 trades/yr.
CAN SLIM: C=Current 25%+. A=Annual. N=New. S=Supply volume. L=Leader RS>80. I=Institutional. M=Market.
LEADERSHIP: Earnings 25%+, RS>90, top industry, institutional, new product. Concentrate 5-8 leaders.
INDUSTRY GROUP: 50% of stock move. Buy top 10-20 groups. First breakout in new theme = leaders.
LOSS AVERSION: 2.5x worse. Holding losers, selling winners. Mechanical rules. Remove emotion.
BASE-ON-BASE: Second base at higher level. Breakout from second = extremely bullish.
IPO LEADERS: No overhead supply, entrepreneurial. Screen <8yr EPS+RS >80+.
MARKET VS LIMIT: Market=fill not price. Limit=price not fill. Liquid=limit. Breakouts=market.
DAILY GRAPHS: Study leaders weekly. Mark buy points, S/R. Hand-marking builds pattern recognition.

SINGLES vs HOME RUNS: 80-90% of P&L from home runs (few/year). But singles FINANCE larger trades — without base hits, pressure to find home runs. Singles = mental clarity, market sharpness, capital to size up. Learn with high frequency (200-300 trades in 6 months). FAST MOVES MEAN REVERT. SLOW MOVES PERSIST. Can't hold fast-mover long-term — setup must be designed for longer hold.

COPY BEFORE YOU CREATE: First task = become profitable. COPY SOMEBODY. Don't reinvent. Pradeep copied 'Hedge Fund Edge' by Mark Boucher — took setup as-is, made it work 2 years, THEN improvised. Actors copy, businesses copy. Take something known to work, replicate, develop your own process through doing. COKE ANALOGY: profitable traders trade 1-2 setups. Coke, Cherry Coke, Vanilla Coke — all the same thing.

FOUR-FACTOR MODEL (When Losing): 1) SETUP — is setup wrong? 2) PROCESS — wrong size, wrong entry, fat finger? 3) MARKET — not in your favor, sit out. 4) TRADER — personal motivation, life events. Success itself drains motivation — 'what am I going to do with the money?' Every year/month starts fresh. NEVER guaranteed to make money. Ever.

content: GAPS AS SUPPORT AND RESISTANCE: Unfilled gaps act as significant support and resistance levels. Breakaway gaps provide the strongest S/R because they start new moves. Runaway gaps offer moderate S/R typically acting as midpoints. Exhaustion gaps provide the weakest S/R and will fill quickly. The volume at the gap determines its S/R strength. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

content: BREAKER BLOCKS: Order block that has been BROKEN — price closed through it. Level flips from support to resistance or vice versa. Powerful because trapped traders from original OB are now underwater. When price returns to a breaker → expect strong reaction. Use as secondary confirmation — FVG aligned with breaker = highest probability entry zone. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

PATTERN FAILURE SIGNALS: A chart pattern that fails to perform its measuring implication is considered a FAILURE and signals a trade in the OPPOSITE direction. A head and shoulders pattern that fails to break the neckline becomes a strong bullish signal. Triangle breakouts that immediately reverse warrant trading in the opposite direction aggressively.

CANDLESTICK DOJI — Open approx Close. Indicates indecision. At top after uptrend = bearish warning. At bottom after downtrend = bullish warning. Types: Long-legged (extreme indecision), Gravestone (bearish at tops - rejection of highs), Dragonfly (bullish at bottoms - rejection of lows). Doji at S/R levels carries more weight. CRYPTO NOTE: Markets 24/7 — session times for CME futures.

ENGULFING CANDLES: Bullish engulfing = green candle's body completely engulfs previous red candle's body. Bearish engulfing = opposite. Engulfing at key level = strong reversal signal. The bigger the engulfing candle relative to previous candles, the stronger the signal. Engulfing + FVG + order block at same level = maximum confluence entry.
